content: HomelearnroboticsLearn robotics engineering  What is it and how to get startedLearn all about the fascinating science of robotics engineering, in which engineers integrate hardware, software, and automation to address real-world challenges.Create an accountBrowse online Robotics CertificatesFind new interests and advance your career opportunitiesStand out in your fieldUse the knowledge and skills you have gained to drive impact at work and grow your career.Learn at your own paceOn your computer, tablet or phone, online courses make learning flexible to fit your busy life.Earn a valuable credentialShowcase your key skills and valuable knowledge.Certificates  9 Courses  92 Executive Education  2 CertificatesRelated TopicsWhat is robotics engineering?Robotics is a branch of engineering focused on creating, developing, and implementing autonomous machines designed to emulate human actions and behaviors.The study of robotics combines principles from mechanical engineering, electrical engineering, and computer science to create systems that interact with the physical world. Robotics has applications in various career fields, including science, engineering, technology, and mathematics.What does a robotics engineer do?Robotics engineers design, build, program, and test automated systems that perform tasks and solve problems. They work with hardware components and systems, such as sensors and motors, as well as software that governs movement, decision-making, and interaction.Robotics engineers must be proficient in Programming languages like Python and C  .Electrical skills, like building motors, sensors, and circuits.Linear algebra.Artificial intelligence skills, like machine learning.Systems thinking skills.Problem-solving skills.Collaborative communication skills.Why learn robotics?Learning robotics can help you Prepare for a stable, high-paying career. According to the Bureau of Labor Statistics  BLS , mechanical engineering jobs, including robotics engineering jobs, are projected to grow by 9  from 2024 to 2034.Enter in-demand sectors like artificial intelligence, aerospace, and advanced manufacturing.Build skills in coding, electronics, and design thinking, combining technical expertise and creativity.In a robotics course, you can learn how intelligent machines sense their environment, process information, and perform physical tasks. From surgical robots to automated agricultural machines, robotics systems shape everyday life. Studying robotics engineering can help you understand these technologies and prepares you to design and build the systems that power modern industries.How to get started in robotics1. Build foundational math and science skillsRobotics engineering requires a foundational knowledge of Computer science  For programming algorithms and understanding data structuresLinear algebra  For calculating the forces and motion involved in robotic movementCalculus  For modeling motion, velocity, and accelerationProbability  For calculating the uncertainty that influences making engineering decisionsPhysics  For understanding mechanical and electrical torque, energy, and circuitsYou can develop these skills by enrolling in individual online courses or pursuing a degree program in a relevant field. The right learning path for you depends on your existing knowledge of the above topics, career goals, financial constraints, and schedule.2. Learn core robotics programming languagesLearning programming languages can help you perform different robotics engineering tasks. Some core programming languages to prioritize learning are Python  Take a single course, like Machine Learning with Python  A Practical Introduction from IBM, or earn a professional certificate in Python to learn the programming language used in robotics for computer vision, prototyping, and AI.C or C    Both C and C   are commonly used in robotics for real-time and performance-intensive tasks, such as motor control and embedded systems. Choose from a single introductory course, like Dartmouth's C Programming  Getting Started, or a professional certificate, like Codio's C   Programming  A Hands-on Introduction program.ROS  While ROS  Robot Operating System  isn't a programming language per se, it's a core robotics framework that integrates Python and C  . Learn the basics of ROS to manage robot communication, simulation, and behavior. Start with an introductory course in robotics, like the Delft University of Technology's Building Robots  Communication Protocols and Hardware.3. Develop problem-solving and engineering skillsProblem-solving and engineering skills are key in the robotics field. If you plan to pursue a career as a mechanical or robotics engineer, you'll want to brush up on Using systems thinking to combine mechanical parts, electronics, and software.Troubleshooting hardware, software, and systems.Strategically designing navigation logic, pathway planning, and task execution.Prototyping robot designs based on real-world performance.You can build these skills during a comprehensive mechanical engineering degree program or through shorter courses and programs in mechanical engineering.4. Develop your own personal robotics projectsA key milestone in your robotics learning journey is developing the ability to apply what you learn to real-world scenarios. Seek opportunities to join clubs, organizations, and competitions to network with like-minded peers and work on projects that you can add to your portfolio.Evidence of successful robotics projects and your ability to work alongside others in the field can help you stand out to employers  and schools, if you plan to eventually apply for a graduate program .5. Pursue entry-level career opportunitiesOnce you feel ready to pursue a career in robotics engineering, the next step is to stand out from the crowd as you pursue employment in the field. One way to achieve this is by earning a professional certificate in robotics. These programs can be completed in 2-10 months and help you build expertise through structured, stackable learning designed for career growth.Some entry-level robotics positions you may qualify for include Junior robotics engineerRobotics technicianJunior automation engineerJunior embedded systems engineerJunior controls engineerJunior software engineerWhat you'll learn in a robotics engineering programTo master robotics engineering, you will need to gain knowledge across multiple disciplines. Foundational robotics courses within a degree program typically cover modeling, kinematics, and equations of motion before progressing to more advanced topics such as planning and control.Other topics covered in robotics engineering courses may include linear problems, machine learning for optimization, and operations research.Most robotics engineering bachelor's programs follow a trajectory similar to the following Year one  The fundamentals of robotics, including calculus, linear algebra, physics, and programmingYear two  Core engineering skill-building in mechanics, circuits, electronics, and control systemsYear three  More advanced courses in kinematics, dynamics, sensors, actuators, and robotics-specific programming within ROSYear four  Specialization opportunities in advanced robotics  e.g., computer vision, AI, healthcare robotics, and industrial robotics , plus a capstone project or internship that demonstrates your understanding of applied roboticsHow many years does it take to become a robotics engineer?According to the BLS, mechanical and robotics engineers typically possess a bachelor's degree in robotics or mechanical engineering. Completing a bachelor's program takes roughly four years. Then, you'll likely need to complete 1-2 years of experiential learning in the form of an internship or entry-level role.If you already have a bachelor's degree in a related field, you may be able to spend 1-2 years taking focused robotics courses, acquiring a professional certificate, or earning a master's degree in mechanical engineering.How to choose the best robotics program for youTo find the right robotics program for you, consider your career goals, interests, time constraints, and budget. edX offers a range of robotics programs designed to fit various student needs, including Courses  Robotics engineering courses are ideal for learners seeking to acquire new skills or deepen their existing knowledge.Professional certificates  Professionals seeking career advancement or a career change should consider earning a professional certificate in robotics engineering. These programs can help you expand your knowledge within the subject while earning credentials recognized by employers.Executive education  Executive education programs are designed for individuals already working in their chosen field who wish to develop leadership or management skills and advance to executive-level positions.Degrees  Pursue a master's degree in engineering or artificial intelligence to build a robust robotics knowledge base and prepare for advanced robotics roles.Jobs that use robotics engineeringRobotics is transforming how humans complete tasks and solve complex problems.Robotics engineers identify problems, design sophisticated solutions, and redefine what's possible in our world. Robotic engineering skills can be applied to multiple career paths and industries, including construction, manufacturing, healthcare, and aerospace.Here are a few positions that you may qualify for with robotics experience, along with salary data from the BLS Robotics engineerComputer and information research scientistSoftware developer, QA analyst testerRobotics technologist technicianRobotics engineerRobotics engineers research, create, and maintain robots and other automated systems. Responsibilities include designing and testing robotic sensors, programming software, and developing solutions that can improve a machine's performance, safety, and reliability.While licensure may not be required for every entry-level mechanical or robotics engineer position, a Professional Engineering  PE  license may be needed as you advance in your career.Required education  Bachelor's degree in mechanical engineering or a related field from an accredited program and state licensureMedian annual salary  May 2024    102,320Robotics engineerRobotics engineers research, create, and maintain robots and other automated systems. Responsibilities include designing and testing robotic sensors, programming software, and developing solutions that can improve a machine's performance, safety, and reliability.While licensure may not be required for every entry-level mechanical or robotics engineer position, a Professional Engineering  PE  license may be needed as you advance in your career.Required education  Bachelor's degree in mechanical engineering or a related field from an accredited program and state licensureMedian annual salary  May 2024    102,320Your next steps to learn robotics online with edXIf you're just starting out, it can be helpful to take some time to explore the field of robotics casually through a short course or program. These shorter pathways can help you learn the fundamentals while discovering the aspects of robotics that interest you the most.edX offers courses and programs from top robotics schools like MIT, Stanford University, and the University of Pennsylvania, which can help you build foundational industry knowledge and experiment with real-world tools to understand how different systems work together. You may want to Take an introductory robotics or free AI course.Explore engineering degrees related to robotics.Build expertise with executive education programs, professional certificates, a master's degree, or MicroMasters  Programs, if you already have a bachelor's degree.Frequently asked questions about roboticsWhat is the salary of a robotics engineer?O NET OnLine reports that robotics engineers earned a median annual salary of  117,750 in 2024. Your salary may vary based on industry, location, and experience.What does a robotics engineer do?A robotics engineer uses software and mechanical hardware to design, build, and test robots that automate human tasks and solve complex problems.Do you need coding for robotics engineering?Coding is an essential skill for a robotics engineer. The code you create provides the instructions robots need to carry out a particular task.Python is one of the most popular programming languages for robotics. While learners don't necessarily need to be expert coders, it's important to have some prior knowledge and be willing to learn.Can you teach yourself robotics?While it's possible to independently learn relevant skills related to robotics, many employers require that applicants hold a bachelor's degree or higher.If you already have a degree in engineering or a related topic, a robotics course or MicroMasters Program can offer additional resources and instructors to support you in learning field-specific skills.Is robotics hard to learn?Robotics requires strong mathematical, programming, and engineering knowledge, which may be challenging for beginners. However, you can get started at any time by enrolling in an online robotics course or an engineering degree program.    context        type    FAQPage ,  mainEntity        type    Question ,  name    What is the salary of a robotics engineer? ,  acceptedAnswer       type    Answer ,  text    O NET OnLine reports that robotics engineers earned a median annual salary of  117,750 in 2024. Your salary may vary based on industry, location, and experience.     ,    type    Question ,  name    What does a robotics engineer do? ,  acceptedAnswer       type    Answer ,  text    A robotics engineer uses software and mechanical hardware to design, build, and test robots that automate human tasks and solve complex problems.     ,    type    Question ,  name    Do you need coding for robotics engineering? ,  acceptedAnswer       type    Answer ,  text    Coding is an essential skill for a robotics engineer. The code you create provides the instructions robots need to carry out a particular task. Python is one of the most popular programming languages for robotics. While learners don't necessarily need to be expert coders, it's important to have some prior knowledge and be willing to learn.     ,    type    Question ,  name    Can you teach yourself robotics? ,  acceptedAnswer       type    Answer ,  text    While it's possible to independently learn relevant skills related to robotics, many employers require that applicants hold a bachelor's degree or higher. If you already have a degree in engineering or a related topic, a robotics course or MicroMasters Program can offer additional resources and instructors to support you in learning field-specific skills.     ,    type    Question ,  name    Is robotics hard to learn? ,  acceptedAnswer       type    Answer ,  text    Robotics requires strong mathematical, programming, and engineering knowledge, which may be challenging for beginners. However, you can get started at any time by enrolling in an online robotics course or an engineering degree program.

metadata.description: Are you interested in breaking into the fascinating world of robotics? Explore courses and programs in robotics to see if this career path is right for you.
